item.title: Google Photos adds a new AI ‘Video Remix’ tool
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The feature can do things like apply cinematic relighting to brighten up a dark clip, swap out a plain background for something fun, or add artistic styles to videos.

item.title: Why this CEO thinks video games make better training data than the internet
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: When it comes to achieving artificial general intelligence (AGI), large language models just don’t have what it takes. Models like ChatGPT and Claude are great at text, but they’re less skilled at understanding how things actually move through space and time — an essential skill for producing intelligence that generalizes. That gap, it turns out, might be filled by gaming data. That’s the bet behind General Intuition, a […]

item.title: Meta wants its AI glasses to seem less creepy. Its AI strategy says otherwise.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Meta is adding a new safeguard to stop people from secretly recording others with its AI glasses. But the update comes as the company continues to expand how much personal data its AI products collect and use.

item.title: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: OpenAI says its new voice mode can speak and listen at the same time, a key ability for live translation.

item.title: Prime Intellect raises $130M Series A to help enterprises build their own AI agents
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Founded in 2024, Prime Intellect’s goal is to give organizations capabilities to train their own agentic systems without relying on frontier AI labs.

item.title: These AI startups are growing revenue at faster and faster rates
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: There are a lot of fast-growing AI startups, but some are growing even faster, they say.

item.title: Your gaming data could be the secret to AGI, according to this Bezos-backed startup
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan, Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: When it comes to achieving artificial general intelligence (AGI), large language models just don’t have what it takes. Models like ChatGPT and Claude are great at text, but they’re less skilled at understanding how things actually move through space and time — an essential skill for producing intelligence that generalizes. That gap, it turns out, might be filled by gaming data. That’s the bet behind General Intuition, a […]

item.title: Former OpenAI exec Kevin Weil is now on the board of Stoke Space
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Kevin Weil's new role at Stoke Space suggests reusable rockets are the next hot thing in Silicon Valley.

item.title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed inference across lots of AI chips
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: ZML, a hot French AI startup endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, has now released ZML/LLMD, software that could make running AI less costly.

item.title: AI chip maker SambaNova raises $1B at $11B valuation, 5 months after last mega round
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: AI chip maker SambaNova has raised at an $11 billion valuation months after Intel was rumored to be trying to buy it for about $1.6 billion.

item.title: Meta just launched a new AI generator, Muse Image, and users are already pushing back over use of their photos
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The new image-generating model has numerous use cases, including advertising and decorating, and creator-based opportunities.

item.title: Why the rise of open source AI isn’t hurting Anthropic … yet
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Open source models’ success isn’t coming at the expense of frontier labs. Instead, they each seem to capture two phases of the same life cycle.

item.title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Microsoft is the latest Silicon Valley giant to cut back on its AI spending.

item.title: Discord admits AI moderation bug wrongfully banned users over harmless images
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: The company confirmed that the issue had been affecting accounts since May, with an additional 200 users banned over the weekend before its team identified and fixed the problem.

item.title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: With this update, users can start a task from their desk, get status updates on their phone, and pick up the finished output later — even if their laptop is closed.

item.title: Savi’s app aims to protect consumers from realistic AI scams like kidnappers demanding ransom
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: The company just raised $7 million in seed funding, and is launching its app for iPhone and Android on Tuesday.

item.title: The first American autonomous ground vehicles are fighting in Ukraine
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Forterra has deployed more than 100 of its self-driving ATVs in conflict zones in Ukraine.

item.title: The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but new details show a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied stolen credentials — meaning it wasn't quite the fully autonomous cybercrime debut that last week's headlines suggested.

item.title: US investors will soon get access to SK Hynix, another memory maker riding the AI boom
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: SK Hynix is experiencing a boom credited to AI. It will ride that to a multibillion-dollar U.S. IPO, expected to take place on Friday.

item.title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch on the fight to split off models from agents
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: "The reality is, when you're optimizing for production, you start looking at a price/performance," Guillermo Rauch tells TechCrunch.
